ollama_format.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

parsed_response.response_text: The mean number of livestock destocked across households begins at zero and rises sharply to 6.0 in the second time step, indicating an immediate reduction in livestock holdings. This initial spike suggests a prompt response to resource constraints around the same time step, driven by climatic variability (rainfall variability of 100) and the risk-averse strategies of E-RO agents. From time step 2 onward, destocking declines gradually but irregularly across households, with values ranging broadly between ~1.5 and ~7.0. Some households experience persistent destocking (e.g., dropping from 7.0 to ~1.5 by time step 29), while others exhibit stabilization or minor adjustments. This divergence reflects heterogeneous resource-seeking behaviors among agents in a simulated environment with fixed rainfall (mean=200) and risky climatic conditions. The gradual decline toward lower average destocking by later time steps aligns with agents' adaptive strategies under resource stress, constrained by the absence of social learning (homog-behav-types?=True) and static parameters like satisficing-threshold (0.0) and resting? (False). By the final recorded time steps, destocking averages remain at ~4.0–5.0 units per column, suggesting persistent but moderated resource management pressures under climatic instability.
